sentences_input.text: And the most unforgettable and remarkable thing that happened then was that this young woman, who had shown me inside, made a sweeping gesture over this whole scene and said the following words, 'Noble be man, merciful and good.' And it was hard for me to believe that she, in that condition, under those circumstances, had been able to summon a line from a poem by the German poet Goethe, a poem called 'The Divine,' because by doing so, she chose to underscore the grim irony of what I was seeing there more effectively than anything else that she might have said.
I talked to some of those women.
One of them, it was pointed out to me later, was a former university professor from Prague, still addressed me in English.
And when I tried to say some words of comfort, she said, oh, I'm sorry.
It's too late, too late for me.
And I found out that she died later that day.

I also found a young girl there, still very pretty but also in terrible condition, who was at that time probably only 15 years old, although the Germans didn't know it, because her sisters had sort of protected her and nursed her along, because she would have been selected as to go among the children who-- who were destined for death if she had given her true age.

But she was 15 years old, and despite the fact that she actually still looked pretty, she went to the hospital as did all the others-- of course, our medics took them immediately to the hospital-- and was there for a few months and still died later that year from the-- what she had gone through on the march.
